name: Hospital License Checklist
shortBenefit: Track all licences required to open a hospital in India.
intro: Ensure your hospital has obtained all statutory licences and registrations required by central and state authorities before commencing operations.

seo.title: Hospital License Checklist — Regulatory Approvals India | Varada Nexus
seo.description: Free hospital licence checklist. Track all regulatory approvals needed to open a hospital in India including PCPNDT, Clinical Establishment, fire NOC and more.

items.text: Society / Trust / Company registration as applicable
Building plan approval from local authority
Electrical safety certificate from licensed inspector
Consent to Operate from State Pollution Control Board
Blood bank licence from CDSCO (if blood bank)
Pharmacy licence from State Drug Authority (if in-house pharmacy)
Radiation safety licence from AERB (if CT / X-ray)
Medical Director with valid MCI/NMC registration
Nursing Home registration / Medical Superintendent appointment

assumptions: Requirements vary by state; central licences (CDSCO, AERB) apply nationally. Verify current norms with local authorities.
disclaimer: This tool provides an indicative estimate for general planning only and is not professional, legal, financial, medical or engineering advice. Reference values are editable defaults that vary by location, vendor, regulation and date. Verify critical figures with a qualified professional. Varada Nexus accepts no liability for decisions made using this tool.

faq.q: Which licence is needed first to open a hospital?
faq.a: The Clinical Establishment registration and building/fire approvals are foundational; without these, no other state licences will be issued.

faq.q: Is PCPNDT registration mandatory for all hospitals?
faq.a: Yes, for any facility that performs ultrasound on pregnant women — including OPD ultrasound — PCPNDT registration is mandatory under law.

faq.q: How long do hospital licences take in India?
faq.a: Timelines vary by state: CEA registration can take 30–90 days; pollution consent 60–120 days; AERB and blood bank licences 3–6 months each.
